skill.name: image-inpainting
skill.description: Mask-driven image inpainting on RunComfy via the runcomfy CLI. Routes to Tongyi MAI Z-Image Turbo Inpainting when a mask is available, and to description-based models when no mask exists. Use for object removal, watermark removal, region replacement, and blemish cleanup.
skill.summary: Remove objects, fill gaps, and replace masked areas in images using the RunComfy CLI with AI inpainting models.
skill.author: agentspace-so

security_audit.summary: All 103 static findings are false positives after manual evaluation. The SKILL.md file is a markdown documentation file with bash command examples, reference URLs, and usage guidance. No executable code exists in the skill. The backtick patterns detected by the static analyzer are Markdown fenced code blocks, not Ruby shell execution. Hardcoded URLs are legitimate documentation links to RunComfy and skills.sh. Hidden file references describe CLI token storage behavior. No weak cryptographic algorithms or system reconnaissance code is present. No prompt injection attempts detected.

content.user_title: Remove Objects and Fill Regions with AI Image Inpainting
content.value_statement: Manually editing images to remove unwanted objects or fill missing areas is time-consuming and requires professional tools. This skill lets you perform mask-driven inpainting through the RunComfy CLI, removing watermarks, objects, and blemishes or replacing entire regions with simple text prompts.
actual_capabilities: Remove unwanted objects from images using a binary mask and AI inpainting.
Remove watermarks from images with mask-driven or description-based fallback.
Replace image regions with AI-generated content via text prompts.
Clean up blemishes and retouch specific areas with adjustable strength.
Route between five different AI models based on mask availability.
Perform brand-style-locked inpainting using LoRA adapters.
limitations: Cannot generate masks. Requires an external tool like Photoshop, GIMP, or a segment-anything model.
Cannot extend canvas beyond original image boundaries. Use an outpainting skill for that task.
Cannot edit video content. This skill is for still images only.
Requires RunComfy CLI to be installed and authenticated before use.

use_cases.title: E-commerce Photo Cleanup
use_cases.description: Remove distracting backgrounds or unwanted objects from product photos without using manual editing software.

use_cases.title: Graphic Design Asset Repair
use_cases.description: Fix damaged or unwanted regions in design assets by masking the area and describing what should appear instead.

use_cases.title: Batch Watermark Removal
use_cases.description: Clean watermarks from reference images by masking the watermark area and applying consistent inpainting across multiple files.
use_cases.target_user: Researchers and data analysts working with visual datasets

prompt_templates.title: Simple Object Removal
prompt_templates.prompt: Remove the [object] from this image using inpainting with a mask. The mask is at [mask_url] and the source image is at [image_url].
prompt_templates.scenario: Basic removal of a single unwanted object using a pre-made mask

prompt_templates.title: Region Replacement with Style
prompt_templates.prompt: Replace the masked area in this image with [description]. Use strength 0.8 and preserve the surrounding style. Image: [image_url], Mask: [mask_url].
prompt_templates.scenario: Replacing a region with specific content while maintaining visual consistency

prompt_templates.title: Description-Based Edit Without Mask
prompt_templates.prompt: Edit this image by [describing the change]. No mask is available, so use Nano Banana 2 Edit to identify the region from my description. Image: [image_url].
prompt_templates.scenario: Editing without a binary mask by describing the target region with spatial language

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Step Professional Retouching
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply a two-step edit: first remove the [object1] at strength 0.5, then replace the [region] with [content] at strength 0.9. Use the same source image for both passes.
prompt_templates.scenario: Complex editing requiring multiple inpainting passes on the same image

output_examples.input: A photo of a street scene with overhead cables that need removal, plus a mask image highlighting the cables.
output_examples.output: The same street scene with the overhead cables cleanly removed and the sky gradient naturally restored in their place.

output_examples.input: A product photo with a cluttered background and a mask covering the background area.
output_examples.output: The same product with a smooth, light gray studio backdrop replacing the cluttered background.

output_examples.input: A reference image with a watermark in the bottom-right corner and a mask covering the watermark.
output_examples.output: The image with the watermark cleanly removed and the underlying content naturally reconstructed.

best_practices: Blur mask edges by 1-3 pixels for smoother transitions between inpainted and preserved regions.
Describe what should stay outside the masked region in the prompt to help the model preserve surrounding content.
Use lower strength values (0.3-0.5) for subtle retouching and higher values (0.8-1.0) for full region replacement.
anti_patterns: Reusing a mask from a different image without verifying it covers the correct region on the target image.
Using inpainting for canvas extension tasks that require an outpainting skill instead.
Using a high strength value for subtle retouching tasks, which can over-edit the masked region.

faq.question: What is image inpainting?
faq.answer: Image inpainting fills or replaces a specific region of an image with AI-generated content. It is commonly used for object removal, watermark removal, and region replacement.

faq.question: Do I need a mask to use this skill?
faq.answer: A mask is recommended for precise control but not required. When no mask is available, the skill uses description-based models that identify the target region from your text prompt.

faq.question: What tools do I need to install?
faq.answer: You need the RunComfy CLI, installed with npm i -g @runcomfy/cli. You also need to sign in with runcomfy login or set a RUNCOMFY_TOKEN environment variable.

faq.question: What image formats are supported?
faq.answer: The skill works with image URLs. Source images and masks must be accessible via URL. Supported formats depend on the underlying RunComfy model endpoints.

faq.question: Can this skill generate masks for me?
faq.answer: No. This skill consumes masks but does not generate them. You need an external tool like Photoshop, GIMP, or a segment-anything model to create binary masks.

faq.question: Can I use this for video editing?
faq.answer: No. This skill is for still image inpainting only. For video inpainting, use the dedicated video-inpainting skill instead.
